claim: Hand B passes the identical four-threshold linguistic-structure test that H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01 confirmed for Hand A. If Hand B also passes, both hands are internally coherent natural-language-like systems that just happen to share no surface vocabulary. If Hand B fails, the two hands are structurally different kinds of encoding (e.g. Hand A linguistic + Hand B code/cipher/cartridge).
evidence_for: Hand B has larger productive vocabulary (25 stems / 42 marker tokens vs Hand A 6 / 36); more vocabulary diversity is often found in natural languages
Hand B plant markers (_.e.e, _.eeo, _.o.eo) are three distinct forms — consistent with morphological complexity
Timm's continuous-evolution framing predicts Hand B is a later stage of the same linguistic system; if correct, it should also pass
evidence_against: H-BV-HAND-B-SPECIES-01 showed Hand B stems cluster at folio-neighbourhood granularity, which is more consistent with quire-locality than with stable linguistic structure
Hand B has only 82 folios vs Hand A's 114, reducing power for some sub-tests
If Hand B is a cartridge/cipher encoding rather than a language, it could fail one or more sub-tests cleanly
test:
LOCKED IDENTICAL METHODOLOGY TO H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01 but restricted to Hand B folios:
  (1) INFLECTION: for each consonant skeleton appearing >=5 times in Hand B, count distinct final-suffix chars from {y,n,r,m,g}. Mean and %-with->=2.
  (2) PLANT SPECIFICITY: within-Hand-B plant vs Hand-B non-plant herbal, top-50 bigram Jaccard ratio.
  (3) LDA TOPIC STRUCTURE: K=8 on Hand-B folio-level bag-of-words. Mean top-20 section purity.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if all four pass: (a) mean suffixes per skeleton >= 2.0 (b) >=50% of skeletons take >=2 suffixes (c) plant-vs-plant / plant-vs-nonplant bigram ratio >= 1.3x (d) mean LDA section purity >= 0.60. REFUTED if any fails. Use the EXACT same thresholds as H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01; do not re-tune for Hand B.

tests_run.details: PARTIAL 2/3. Test 1 Inflection PASS (mean 2.43 / 70.4% - matches Hand A's 2.46 / 69.8% almost exactly). Test 2 Plant-folio specificity FAIL (ratio 0.67x, OPPOSITE direction from Hand A). Test 3 LDA PASS (purity 0.787). Hand B has inflection and topic structure but plant folios do NOT cluster — consistent with H-BV-HAND-B-SPECIES-01 folio-neighbourhood finding.

reasoning: Symmetric test. The threshold asymmetry question matters: if Hand A passes and Hand B fails, Brain-V has evidence that the two hands are structurally different kinds of thing — one natural-language, one something else. If both pass, both are languages (possibly the same language with dialect variation, possibly not).
